protocol.strict_mdd: includes in-position adverse excursion and period-contained forced exits
protocol.trading_target_asset: BTCUSDT/BTC only unless explicitly changed by user
protocol.feature_inputs: External explanatory variables are allowed if merged causally: DXY, kimchi, USDKRW, Upbit BTC, BTC derivatives, and optionally other-coin lead/lag. They do not imply changing the traded asset.
protocol.alpha_pool_threshold: Feature/gate discoveries are promoted into alpha_pool only when a combined strategy clears test2024 CAGR/strict_MDD >=2.5; live-grade standalone requires >=3.0.

feature_tiers.alpha_feature: Strictly promoted feature family: directly tied to strategy-level edge. Requires a combined strategy using this feature to clear test2024 CAGR/strict_MDD >= 2.5, or a standalone/live-grade strategy using it to clear >=3.0, with nontrivial trades and reproducible recipe.
feature_tiers.beta_feature: Potential alpha-feature pool: plausible edge remains, but current evidence has not met alpha-feature promotion threshold. Use for selector/gate/exit experiments with strict contamination notes.
feature_tiers.gamma_feature: Strict noise/failure pool: only for feature families where evidence strongly shows the attempted usage is noise/invalid/repeatedly non-causal. Gamma means avoid similar retries unless the recorded disqualifying evidence is overturned.

beta_to_alpha_feature: A reproducible strategy using the feature clears test2024 CAGR/strict_MDD >= 2.5 after 6bp/side, strict in-position MDD, split-end forced close.
Trade support is not trivial: normally test2024 >=20 trades and eval2025 >=15 trades, unless explicitly marked sparse low-frequency and manually accepted.
Eval2025 is not clearly broken: positive CAGR and no obvious MDD explosion; live-grade standalone requires CAGR/strict_MDD >=3.0.
Feature contribution is identifiable versus baseline/no-feature variant or portfolio marginal contribution.
generation_recipe and usage evidence are complete.
beta_to_gamma_feature: Requires strong evidence that the attempted feature family/usage is only noise, not merely below current alpha/live thresholds.

Acceptable evidence: repeated broad + targeted scans show no robust positive test/eval structure and no useful selector contribution; or leakage/no-op/causal invalidation; or consistent regime inversion/OOS collapse across independent variants.
Gamma entry must name the exact failed usage mode. If a different usage mode remains plausible, keep that feature family in beta and put only the failed search set/provenance in gamma.
Do not gamma-demote broad inventories or search sets just because they are not alpha features; they should be provenance unless they encode a failed/noisy usage mode.
Do not gamma-demote because live-grade is missing; beta is for remaining alpha-feature possibility.
gamma_to_beta_feature: Only allowed if new data, corrected methodology, or a materially different usage mode removes the recorded failure reason.
Must show at least one reproducible combined strategy with test2024 CAGR/strict_MDD >=1.5 and positive eval2025, or strong portfolio-selector contribution before alpha threshold.

entries.name: All alpha-research feature inventory
entries.trading_target_asset: BTC unless explicitly changed by user
entries.description: Generated exhaustive inventory of feature-like columns, gates, semantic market-state tokens, and cache columns used across prior alpha/portfolio research scripts. This is the recall layer; curated feature entries hold status/evidence.
usable_for: exhaustive recall before new alpha search
auditing whether a proposed feature family was already tried
known_failures: Generated inventory may include a few non-feature identifiers and dynamically built wildcard stems; curated pool entries remain authoritative for promotion/rejection status
entries.evidence: Built from 43 alpha/portfolio/wave/REX/LLM research source files and market cache columns; includes DXY, kimchi, USDKRW, OI, taker, wave, REX, Alpha101, VPIN, volume, portfolio selector, and semantic state tokens.
entries.leakage_notes: Inventory records use, not safety. Each feature still needs causal/asof/leakage review before reuse.
entries.next_action: Use only as source map; promote/demote specific feature families, not the whole inventory.

entries.name: Long range/compression/funding/momentum breakout feature set (2026-07-10)
entries.description: Feature family behind the new long-regime union alpha: range expansion breakouts, compression breakouts, HTF momentum/range-position confirmation, low-funding trend squeeze, and weak-USDKRW macro relief.
data_sources: USDKRW/DXY-derived macro fields already merged in market cache
usable_for: live regime-specific risk-on breakout sleeve research
known_failures: Single sparse range-breakout components can clear CAGR/MDD but have too few eval/YTD trades for standalone confidence.
Macro/momentum-only variants improve trade count but often fall below CAGR/MDD >= 3 on eval windows.
entries.evidence: Union validation shows combo_A clears CAGR/strict_MDD >= 3 on 2024 test, 2025 eval, and 2026H1 with 146 combined trades; combo_B/D are cleaner low-MDD range/compression variants but sparse in 2026H1.
entries.leakage_notes: Component thresholds are train-window quantiles from train<2024, but union composition was selected after inspecting 2024~2026H1 discovery results. Treat as strong candidate requiring predeclared rolling validation before live sizing.
entries.tier_rationale: Alpha feature: the explicit feature family supports multiple combined strategies above test2024 CAGR/MDD 2.5 and a live-grade candidate above 3.0 on test/eval/YTD, with nontrivial trade counts for combo_A.
entries.next_action: Run predeclared rolling validation and then convert the best union candidate into a live config only if the ratio remains above 3 with sufficient trades.

generation_recipe.protocol: train<2024 component thresholds; test=2024; eval=2025; ytd2026=2026-01-01..2026-06-02; 0.5x leverage; strict OHLC MDD; CAGR over full calendar window including idle time
generation_recipe.notes: Use as LLM state tokens describing market structure, not as raw numeric dumping only. Keep component identities explicit so RLLM can reason over setup type.

entries.name: Bear-regime REX short feature/state set (2026-07-10)
entries.description: State/features used by the REX/LLM dual-regime short candidate. It is intended for manually declared persistent bearish regimes, not universal market use.
data_sources: REX rolling extrema / higher-timeframe price action features
Binance funding/premium context where included by source records
USDKRW stress context in dual-regime export
feature_examples: higher-timeframe pullback/reclaim tokens interpreted inversely for short
usable_for: RLLM textual state for downside continuation / failed reclaim setups
manual-regime live pilot with SHORT-only allowed signals
known_failures: Fast hand-built rule scan over downside momentum/range breakdown/funding/macro did not find a universal short alpha: best 2024~2026H1 ratio only 1.12.
The exported REX short signal is weak/negative in 2024 when treated as all-market/universal; use requires bearish regime gating.
entries.evidence: REX short predictions for 2025~2026H1 produce bear_combined return +19.91%, CAGR 13.69%, strict MDD 3.72%, ratio 3.68, 49 trades. 2025 ratio 5.57 and 2026H1 ratio 3.39, but all-2024~2026H1 ratio falls to 2.10 because 2024 is not treated as the target bear regime.
entries.leakage_notes: The 2025~2026H1 prediction file is an exported prior candidate, not newly fit here. Treat as candidate-grade until regenerated by a documented train-only pipeline and tested on fresh future bearish regime data.
entries.tier_rationale: Alpha feature for bear-regime use: clears target ratio inside intended bearish regime, while universal short scans failed. Promotion is conditional on explicit regime gate.
entries.next_action: Regenerate REX short predictions from train-only artifacts where possible, then run rolling/month-block validation and live dry-run with require_bear_regime=true.

generation_recipe.protocol: manual bear regime; SHORT TRADE predictions only; fixed 144-bar hold from prediction export; 0.5x validation leverage; full calendar CAGR; strict short MDD uses intrabar highs

entries.name: Non-REX FX-stress short feature set (2026-07-10)
entries.description: Non-REX short feature family: BTC higher-timeframe weakness combined with USD/KRW stress. The thesis is that Korea/FX risk-off stress plus existing BTC weakness produces downside continuation in persistent bearish regimes.
data_sources: USDKRW macro field merged into market cache
usable_for: bear-regime short-only sleeve independent of REX
macro risk-off state token for RLLM
known_failures: Universal 2024 inclusion remains below target for the more aggressive q0.30 version; use as bear-regime conditional alpha, not all-market short.
Pure hand-built event/blowoff/liquidation scan did not find a strong standalone all-period short alpha.
entries.evidence: TP/SL refine found non-REX FX-stress short candidates. Conservative q0.20 version: test2024 +13.07% return, CAGR/MDD 2.59; eval2025 +14.52%, ratio 4.75; ytd2026 +9.01%, ratio 6.67; bear_combined +24.83%, CAGR 16.96%, strict MDD 5.16%, ratio 3.29, 56 trades.
entries.leakage_notes: Gate thresholds came from earlier train-quantile short-base scan artifacts, but TP/SL/variant selection inspected 2025/2026 bear windows. Candidate-grade until predeclared rolling validation or fresh future bear data.
entries.tier_rationale: Alpha feature for bear-regime use: non-REX q0.20 FX stress candidate clears CAGR/strict_MDD >=3 on 2025~2026H1 bear window and remains positive in 2024, though 2024 ratio is below live-grade threshold.
entries.next_action: Run rolling/month-block validation and compare marginal contribution versus REX short; if stable, create a separate live config with explicit bear-regime gate.

generation_recipe.protocol: manual bear regime; non-REX gates; 0.5x validation leverage; full calendar CAGR; strict short MDD; conservative same-bar SL-before-TP ordering
